steps.title: Calculate the original mean
steps.content:
Sum the five egg counts from the table:
$$149 + 144 + 148 + 136 + 139 = 716$$
Divide by 5 to find the original mean:
$$\text{Original mean} = \frac{716}{5} = 143.2$$

steps.title: Calculate the new mean and compare
steps.content:
Adding the sixth nest with 121 eggs, the sum becomes $716 + 121 = 837$. With 6 nests, the new mean is:
$$\text{New mean} = \frac{837}{6} = 139.5$$
Comparing: $143.2 > 139.5$. The original mean is greater than the new mean. That matches choice A — A is correct.
